scenarios.description: Single-record Put+Get+Delete round-trip; Get after Delete returns not-found.
assertions: Get(fileID) after Put returns a record deep-equal to rec
Put again with changed fields (overwrite) then Get returns the NEW value (proves Put overwrites, no versioning)
Get(fileID) after Delete returns an error satisfying errors.Is(err, ErrNotFound)

scenarios.description: Concurrent Put/Get/Delete across many distinct fileIDs from many goroutines, -race clean, no corruption.
assertions: 300 distinct fileIDs (> numStripes=256, exercising every stripe), each with 4 concurrent goroutines doing Put then Get, run under go test -race with no data race reported
Every concurrent Get (racing against other workers' Puts for the same fileID, all writing an identical value) either errors or returns exactly the expected value - no torn/partial reads
After all concurrent Puts/Gets complete, a final sequential Get for every one of the 300 fileIDs deep-equals what was Put (no lost updates, no corruption)
All 300 fileIDs concurrently Deleted from separate goroutines, each returns nil error
After concurrent Delete, every fileID's Get returns errors.Is(err, ErrNotFound)

scenarios.description: Stripe-contention: operations on fileIDs in different stripes must not serialize behind one lock.
assertions: Directly acquire (in-package test access) the record-stripe mutex for heldFileID and hold it open-ended via a channel signal
Assertion A: Put on a fileID hashing to a DIFFERENT stripe completes within 2s (does not wait for the held lock's release) - proves cross-stripe non-serialization

Assertion B (control): Get on a fileID hashing to the SAME stripe as the held lock does NOT complete within 150ms while the lock is held - proves the stripe lock is a real lock (necessary control so Assertion A is meaningful, not vacuously true because stripes do nothing)
After releasing the held lock, the same-stripe Get completes within 2s

scenarios.description: Get/Delete on a nonexistent fileID returns the expected not-found error (not panic, not nil-without-error).
assertions: Get(missing fileID) returns a non-nil error satisfying errors.Is(err, ErrNotFound)
Delete(missing fileID) returns a non-nil error satisfying errors.Is(err, ErrNotFound)

additional_test.description: Sanity check that stripeFor(fileID) is deterministic and spans all numStripes=256 distinct stripes across a range of fileIDs; a building-block correctness check supporting scenario 3's test setup, not itself one of the 4 required scenarios.

overall_coverage: 4/4 required scenarios covered by dedicated test functions; all pass under go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestCatalog -race -v
